paper name: YOTO (You Only Think Once): A Human EEG Dataset for Multisensory Perception and Mental Imagery
authors: Yan-Han Chang; Hsi-An Chen; Min-Jiun Tsai; Chun-Lung Tseng; Ching-Huei Lo; Kuan-Chih Huang; Chun-Shu Wei
paradigm: Unimodal visual + unimodal auditory + audiovisual combinations + imagery
paper description: Multisensory EEG resource for perception and imagery with factorial AV combinations.
dataset description: Contains tones (C,D,E), vowels, visual stimuli, and combined conditions suitable for modality and pitch-class tasks.
additional information: Strong base for your plan; visual stream is not color-rich (grayscale/faces), so pair with a dedicated color dataset.

paper name: Decoding visual colour from scalp electroencephalography measurements
authors: Jasper E. Hajonides; Anna C. Nobre; Freek van Ede; Mark G. Stokes
paradigm: Bilateral Gabor gratings with 48 CIELAB hues (L=54), short presentations
paper description: Shows robust EEG color decoding (e.g., 12 bins, posterior signals, ~100-350 ms).
dataset description: High-value hue-decoding dataset with controlled color space.
additional information: Best direct color dataset for pretraining/fine-tuning color-attention branches before fusion with auditory data.

paper name: Decoding of the neural representation of the visual RGB color model
authors: Yijia Wu; Yanjing Mao; Kaiqiang Feng; Donglai Wei; Liang Song
paradigm: Rapid visual RGB stimulation (red/green/blue) with SVM decoding
paper description: Demonstrates 3-class RGB decoding with EEG in a simple paradigm.
dataset description: Compact and practical dataset for RGB classification baselines.
additional information: Useful for quick pilot of Labram classification head and augmentation tests with clear labels.

paper name: Bae & Luck color working memory decoding studies (OSF jnwut)
paper description: EEG decoding in working memory paradigms with stimulus-specific representations.
dataset description: Includes color-wheel style tasks with analysis scripts.
additional information: Good if you want Labram to generalize beyond immediate perception into maintenance/imagery-like states.

paper name: Decoding of EEG signals reveals non-uniformities in the neural geometry of colour
authors: Pallav M. Chauhan; Ivan Jakovljev; Simon B. Thompson; Sophie Wuerger; Jasna Martinovic
paradigm: Unique and intermediate hue decoding with representational analyses
paper description: Finds anisotropic neural hue geometry and decodable unique hues.
dataset description: Color-focused EEG dataset suitable for representational and classifier experiments.
additional information: Strong candidate if you want richer hue geometry for fine-tuning than simple RGB tasks.

paper name: Analyzing the Recognition of Color Exposure and Imagined Color from EEG Signals
paper description: Examines EEG-based recognition of seen vs imagined RGB colors.
dataset description: Consumer-grade EEG dataset family for real/imagined color tasks.
additional information: Data access may require author contact; lower channel count but useful for imagination condition alignment with your plan.

paper name: The Nencki-Symfonia EEG/ERP dataset: Multiple cognitive tasks and resting-state data
authors: Patrycja Dzianok; Ingrida Antonova; Jakub Wojciechowski; Joanna Dreszer; Ewa Kublik
paradigm: 3-stimulus auditory oddball + additional cognitive tasks + rest
paper description: Open high-density multi-task EEG/ERP dataset including auditory oddball.
dataset description: Provides robust auditory labels (standard/target/distractor) and larger sample size.
additional information: Useful for auditory branch pretraining before combining with color datasets.

paper name: Diminished EEG habituation to novel events effectively classifies Parkinson's patients
authors: James F. Cavanagh; Praveen Kumar; Andrea A. Mueller; Sarah Pirio Richardson; Abdullah Muen
paradigm: 3-stimulus auditory oddball with PD and control participants
paper description: Shows oddball-derived EEG signatures discriminating PD vs controls.
dataset description: Contains auditory oddball EEG with repeated sessions ON/OFF meds for PD group.
additional information: Use healthy controls only for cleaner generalization; clinical confounds otherwise likely hurt base model transfer.

paper name: A multi-subject, multi-modal human neuroimaging dataset (auditory/visual oddball EEG-fMRI)
authors: Paul Sajda; Truman R. Brown; Bryan Conroy; Jordan Muraskin; Robin I. Goldman; Jennifer M. Walz
paradigm: Matched auditory and visual oddball tasks with simultaneous EEG-fMRI
paper description: Multimodal oddball dataset supporting cross-modal attention analyses.
dataset description: Includes both auditory and visual streams in same participants.
additional information: Good for modality classification; EEG quality considerations due to MRI artifacts even after correction.

paper name: EEG-based BCI Dataset of Semantic Concepts for Imagination and Perception Tasks
authors: Holly Wilson; Mohammad Golbabaee; Michael Proulx; Eamonn O'Neill
paradigm: 3 semantic concepts across 3 modalities (audio/text/image), perception and imagination
paper description: Open semantic EEG dataset across modalities for BCI decoding.
dataset description: Useful for multimodal and imagination-focused modeling.
additional information: Not color-specific, but relevant for multimodal fusion and transfer to attended audiovisual tasks.
